cells.rationale: Liveness-aware close-claim arbitration is a subtle concurrency/correctness invariant: a wrong recycle-safe pid+start-time probe can misclassify a live closer as dead (double-close) or fail to release a killed holder (deadlock). Bumps the marker schema with backward-compat and defer-on-inconclusive semantics — contract-shaped, must be validated not just implemented. Nameable intelligence-bound reason clears the opus burden; xhigh for the schema/contract touch.

Cross-module threading of a marker-clear helper into the terminate kill path plus daemon reaper/cascade owner-terminal paths, requiring discovery ('locate the CLI/daemon impl') and a security-sensitive judgment (clear only on positively-confirmed death, never on session_identity_unproven/command_unowned refusals). Well-specified in intent with task 1 as the correctness backstop, so opus isn't warranted, but the refusal-path distinction and multi-surface navigation want Claude judgment over a GPT tier; high fits a known-pattern helper thread across a few files.
